definition: Construct an approximate k-nearest-neighbor graph by iteratively improving candidate neighbor lists using the neighbors-of-neighbors principle. This is useful when SPECTRA needs a neighbor graph rather than independent point queries, especially for large embedding tables or custom distances.

scale_profile.best_for: Large datasets where the audit can use a kNN graph over train/eval points and exact all-pairs computation is infeasible.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions of points in optimized CPU/GPU implementations.
scale_profile.avoid_when: Exact pairwise distances are required for all threshold levels and no recall calibration subset is available.

required_inputs: vector features or pairwise distance callback

tunable_parameters.name: k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of neighbors retained per point.

tunable_parameters.name: sample_rate
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls candidate expansion cost and recall.

quality_gates: Measure recall@k against exact neighbors on a representative subset.
Report k, iterations, and convergence diagnostics.
Check that train-eval neighbor extraction is not contaminated by eval labels.
failure_modes: Approximate graph errors can make samples appear spuriously novel.
Small k can miss medium-distance neighborhoods needed for low-overlap SPECTRA bins.
Graph construction can converge poorly when similarity is noisy or non-metric.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py as an exact small-scale contract; production NN-Descent implementations should emit the same pairwise_similarity.csv columns.

citations.title: Fast Single-Core K-Nearest Neighbor Graph Computation
citations.evidence: Optimizes NN-Descent for k-nearest-neighbor graph computation and reports substantial runtime improvements.

citations.title: Large-Scale Approximate k-NN Graph Construction on GPU
citations.evidence: Adapts NN-Descent for GPU-based large-scale kNN graph construction.
